calendarFaqs.q: When does school start for Plano ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The first day of school for Plano ISD is Tuesday, August 11, 2026. Students and staff are expected to report on this date to begin the new academic year — one day earlier than the 2025–2026 start date of August 12.

calendarFaqs.q: When is the last day of school for Plano ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The last day of school for Plano ISD is Friday, May 21, 2027. The school year runs from August through May, covering 171 instructional days.

calendarFaqs.q: When is fall break 2026-2027 for Plano ISD?
calendarFaqs.a: Fall break for Plano ISD runs October 12–16, 2026 — a full week off. Dallas ISD and most other DFW neighbors remain in session that week, so this is a distinctly Plano break that often catches families by surprise when coordinating childcare.

calendarFaqs.q: When is spring break 2026-2027 for Plano ISD?
calendarFaqs.a: Spring break for Plano ISD runs March 15–19, 2027, aligning with Dallas ISD and Fort Worth ISD. Two additional no-school days follow: a student/teacher holiday on March 26 and a staff day on March 29.

calendarFaqs.q: When is winter break 2026-2027 for Plano ISD?
calendarFaqs.a: Winter break for Plano ISD runs December 21, 2026–January 4, 2027 (15 days). January 4 is a staff day; second semester begins Tuesday, January 5, 2027.

yearNumbers.label: Starts Earlier
yearNumbers.detail: First day moves from Aug 12 to Aug 11 vs 2025–2026.

yearNumbers.label: Fall Break
yearNumbers.detail: Oct 12–16 full-week fall break continues — a Plano ISD hallmark that neighboring districts including Dallas ISD do not offer.

yearNumbers.label: Good Friday Dropped
yearNumbers.detail: Good Friday is no longer on the calendar; instead Mar 26 (Student/Teacher Holiday) and Mar 29 (Staff Day) replace the single-day April observance.

calendarNotes: Plano ISD begins the 2026–2027 school year on August 11, 2026, and the year ends May 21, 2027. Unlike many Dallas-area districts, PISD schedules a full five-day Fall Break from October 12–16 — neighboring districts including Dallas ISD remain in session that week, which makes PISD's Fall Break one of the less congested travel windows on the Texas school calendar. An additional no-school day falls on November 3 (Staff Day), followed by Thanksgiving Break running November 23–27; the first two days are Professional Learning Trade Days, extending what would otherwise be a two-day holiday into a full five-day closure. Winter Break begins December 21 — December 18 is an early-release day — with January 4 as a Staff Day and January 5 as the first day of second semester. Spring Break runs March 15–19, aligning with Dallas ISD and Fort Worth ISD; all three districts releasing the same week concentrates demand at DFW International Airport and pushes hotel and flight prices across the state. Two no-school days follow in late March: the 26th (Student/Teacher Holiday) and the 29th (Staff Day). For most PISD households, October Fall Break and the post–Spring Break stretch in late March are the two periods most likely to fall outside standard planning assumptions.
